phrases.intent_en: Where is the tourist information desk inside
phrases.text_local: Kde je uvnitř turistické informační centrum?
phrases.cultural_note: Town halls often house a small tourist info point in the lobby.

likely_response.local: V přízemí, hned za hlavním vchodem.
likely_response.translation_en: On the ground floor, right behind the main entrance.

responses.english: On the ground floor, right behind the main entrance.
responses.local: V přízemí, hned za hlavním vchodem.

phrases.intent_en: Must I remove my hat in the council chamber
phrases.text_local: Musím si v zasedací síni sundat klobouk?
phrases.cultural_note: Removing hats indoors, especially in formal rooms, is standard Czech etiquette.

phrases.intent_en: How do I book the wedding hall
phrases.text_local: Jak si můžu zarezervovat obřadní síň?
phrases.cultural_note: Wedding hall bookings are handled by the matrika (registry office), not a general reception.

likely_response.local: Vyplňte žádost na matrice a přineste doklady.
likely_response.translation_en: Fill out a request at the registry office and bring your documents.

responses.english: Fill out a request at the registry office and bring your documents.
responses.local: Vyplňte žádost na matrice a přineste doklady.

phrases.intent_en: When is the clock tower open for visitors
phrases.text_local: Kdy je otevřená věž s hodinami pro návštěvníky?
phrases.cultural_note: Tower hours are often limited and separate from the main building hours.

phrases.intent_en: Where do I buy guided tour tickets
phrases.text_local: Kde si můžu koupit lístky na prohlídku?
phrases.cultural_note: Czech ticket offices are typically separate from the main reception.

likely_response.translation_en: At the ticket office to the right of the entrance.

responses.english: At the ticket office to the right of the entrance.
